source_jobs.title: Supply Chain Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
At Pair Eyewear, we believe in placing our people at the center of everything we do. We are dedicated to fostering a joyful and dynamic workplace, providing our employees with an environment where they can thrive and make a meaningful impact.



We are seeking a Supply Chain Associate to support both inbound and outbound logistics operations, ensuring smooth execution, visibility, and reporting across the supply chain. This role will report into the Senior Manager of Logistics Operations and work with cross-functional partners to manage purchase order flow, freight execution, 3PL coordination, SKU management, and issue resolution—helping ensure product moves efficiently from suppliers to warehouses and ultimately to customers and partners, even during peak periods and key launches.



What You’ll Do:

 - Manage inbound purchase orders and shipments, ensuring suppliers meet ship dates, quantities, and documentation, while proactively flagging risks and maintaining reporting for supply planning and warehouse visibility

 - Coordinate inbound freight bookings and track shipments through delivery, resolving delays, shortages, damages, or missing paperwork

 - Execute and track outbound shipments, including 3PL coordination, replacements, and issue resolution

 - Partnering with warehouse teams to ensure timely execution, especially during peak periods, launches, and high-volume seasons

 - Own SKU setup and ongoing maintenance in NetSuite, ensuring item data accuracy across supply chain–critical fields

 - Prepare weekly and monthly KPI reporting on PO health, inbound/outbound performance, transit times, and service levels

 - Support cross-functional handoffs and process improvements across Sourcing, Planning, CX, Logistics, and warehouse partners

 - Manage ad hoc, rush, or exception-based shipments as needed



Who You Are:

 - Highly organized and execution-focused, with the ability to manage multiple inbound and outbound shipments, POs, and SKUs simultaneously

 - Comfortable working with large data sets and maintaining accuracy across detailed trackers and reports

 - Detail-oriented with a strong focus on data hygiene, system accuracy, and follow-through

 - Able to prioritize tasks in a fast-paced environment and adapt as supply chain timelines shift

 - Proactive in identifying and escalating risks related to timing, quantities, or system data integrity

 - Comfortable working cross-functionally with internal teams, suppliers, freight forwarders, 3PLs, and warehouse partners

 - Clear, concise communicator who is comfortable asking questions, documenting issues, and escalating when needed



Need to Have:

 - Bachelor’s degree in Business, Supply Chain, Operations, or a related field

 - 3-4 years of experience in supply chain, logistics, sourcing, production, or operations

 - Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel (e.g., VLOOKUPs, pivot tables, data analysis)

 - Experience working with freight forwarders, carriers, and distribution centers or 3PLs

 - Hands-on NetSuite experience (required), including SKU/item management and supply chain–related workflows

 - Eagerness to learn and grow within a fast-paced, scaling supply chain environment



Nice to have:

 - Experience working with SourceDay and a range of TMS tools to support PO management, freight tracking, and logistics coordination




SOME BENEFITS AND PERKS OF WORKING AT PAIR:

 - Health, vision and dental insurance

 - Generous vacation policies

 - Competitive salaries with equity opportunities

 - Retirement savings plan options

 - Free eyewear (plus discounts for family and friends)



At Pair, we carefully consider a wide range of factors when determining compensation. These considerations can cause your compensation to vary. The pay range for this position is expected to be between $70,000 - $75,000 and will depend on your skills, qualifications, and experience. Additionally, this role might be eligible for discretionary short-term and long-term incenti

source_jobs.title: Rx-Lab Coating Lead
source_jobs.description:
At Pair Eyewear, we believe in placing our people at the center of everything we do. We are dedicated to fostering a joyful and dynamic workplace, providing our employees with an environment where they can thrive and make a meaningful impact.



Pair is seeking a Rx Lab Coating Lead hiring for our prescription eyewear manufacturing department in Irvine, CA. This role will report to our Coating Supervisor.



What You’ll Do:

 - You will maintain company Health and Safety standards including PPE (where applicable)

 - You will have a lead role in training of all new Coating Operator associates

 - You will support the Rx-Lab production team in driving flawless execution of the Coating department, support the implementation of standard operating procedures (SOP) to ensure efficient and effective processes in order to maximize productivity

 - You will communicate clear priorities and productivity goals to Coating Department Associates and hold team and self-accountable for achieving them

 - You will provide management with productivity updates; proactively identify and communicate feedback on Associate performance

 - You will troubleshoot production errors, while providing constructive feedback to team

 - You will partner with other area leaders, ensuring continuity amongst the broader group exist

 - You will interact daily with production supervisor, QA, maintenance, and other groups to keep operations running smoothly and properly

 - You will coordinate the daily prioritization of production order(s) within the Coating Department, escalating any and all delays

 - You will receive and put away replenishment inventory in a timely manner

 - You will provide project support within assigned area

 - You will provide objective feedback and coaching on performance to staff and shares feedback as required

 - Support other Production/Operations (Print/Logistics, Stockroom, Coating) on an as needed basis

 - You will maintain excellent working relationships with other area/department leaders

 - You will analyze daily production metrics, takes a proactive approach for implementing improvements in a timely manner



Who You Are:

 - You are detailed and organized

 - You have previous experience in similar industry

 - You are an effective communicator and capable of leading with empathy

 - You have excellent multitasking skills

 - You are comfortable working in a fast-paced light manufacturing environment

 - You are self-motivated and an efficient problem solver



Need to Have:

 - 2+ years of experience in a production environment leading teams

 - A proven track record meeting/exceeding expectations

 - Good practical experience with Google Suite

 - Basic computer knowledge

 - Strong organizational skills, together with a results-driven approach

 - Strong communication and interpersonal skills

 - High School Diploma

 - Available to work on site full time



Nice to Have:

 - Experience with Schneider Optical Machinery

 - Experience with DVI (Digital Vision Inc), LMS (Lab Management System)

 - Experience providing operational training to others



Some Benefits and Perks of Working At Pair:

 - Health, vision and dental insurance

 - Generous vacation policies

 - Competitive salaries with equity opportunities

 - Retirement savings plan options

 - Free eyewear (plus discounts for family and friends)

At Pair, we carefully consider a wide range of factors when determining compensation. These considerations can cause your compensation to vary. The pay range for this position is expected to be between $23.00 - $31.00 and will depend on your skills, qualifications, and experience. Additionally, this role might be eligible for discretionary short-term and long-term incentives. We encourage all interested candidates to apply.


Pair is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment and will not be discriminated against on the basis of actual or perceived age, ancestry, c

source_jobs.title: Creative Director
source_jobs.description:
At Pair Eyewear, we believe in placing our people at the center of everything we do. We are dedicated to fostering a joyful and dynamic workplace, providing our employees with an environment where they can thrive and make a meaningful impact.



We’re looking for a Creative Director to lead and evolve the Pair Eyewear brand across every touchpoint. This role will report to our VP of Marketing and sits the intersection of brand, performance, and product storytelling, shaping how we show up visually and creatively as we continue to scale.

You’ll be responsible for defining and driving our creative vision, leading a high-performing team, and ensuring everything we put into the world feels cohesive, thoughtful, and distinctly Pair. This is a strategic and hands-on role, you'll be setting the creative vision and roadmap one moment, and in the weeds giving art direction on a shoot or refining copy the next. From campaign concepting to execution, you’ll balance big-picture brand building with performance-driven creative that delivers results.



What You’ll Do

Creative Direction & Strategy

 - Define and evolve Pair Eyewear’s creative vision across all channels, ensuring a cohesive and differentiated brand

 - Partner with Marketing, Product, and Leadership to align creative strategy with key business goals and growth priorities

Campaign Development

 - Lead the concepting and execution of integrated campaigns across paid, owned, and earned channels

 - Own creative vision and direction for photoshoots, video, product launches, and key brand moments

Team Leadership & Development

 - Build, lead, and develop a high-performing creative team across design, copy, content, and production
   Provide clear direction, thoughtful feedback, and mentorship to elevate both individual and team output

Operations, Process & Resourcing

 - Own creative resourcing, prioritization, and budget allocation across initiatives

 - Establish and refine processes that improve speed, quality, and cross-functional collaboration

 - Manage external partners, freelancers, and agencies as needed

Brand Systems & Consistency

 - Maintain and evolve brand guidelines and creative standards to ensure consistency across all touchpoints

 - Champion a high bar for creative quality while enabling speed and iteration



Who You Are

 - You’re a strategic creative leader who understands how creative drives both brand and business impact

 - You balance brand and performance, creating work that is visually compelling, emotionally resonant, and results-driven

 - You have a strong point of view on creative and are confident advocating for it, while collaborating effectively across teams

 - You’re data-informed, using insights and testing to continuously improve creative performance

 - You’re a builder, excited to develop scalable creative systems, processes, and a high-performing team

 - You’re operationally strong, able to manage priorities, move quickly, and deliver high-quality work

 - You’re a people-first leader who develops talent, gives clear feedback, and raises the bar for creative output



Need to Have

 - 8+ years of experience in creative roles, with experience leading teams (in-house, agency, or both)

 - Proven experience developing and executing integrated marketing campaigns across multiple channels

 - Strong portfolio demonstrating creative direction across brand, digital, and physical experiences

 - Experience working with performance marketing teams and understanding how creative drives results

 - Experience leveraging AI tools (e.g., generative image, video, and copy tools) to accelerate creative production, testing velocity, and workflow efficiency

 - Exceptional visual design sensibility, attention to detail, and storytelling ability

 - Experience leading photoshoots, video production, and large-scale campaign execution

 - Strong leadership and team management experience

 - Familiarity with tools like Figma, Adobe Creative Suite, and c

source_jobs.title: Paid Search/SEO Manager
source_jobs.description:
At Pair Eyewear, we believe in placing our people at the center of everything we do. We are dedicated to fostering a joyful and dynamic workplace, providing our employees with an environment where they can thrive and make a meaningful impact.



Pair Eyewear is seeking a Paid Search/SEO Manager to own the full search channel from campaign architecture, keyword strategy, and bid management across Search, Shopping, and Performance Max, to ad copy testing, feed optimization, and conversion tracking. There will be a strong emphasis on the analytical side: hitting customer acquisition cost targets, evaluating attribution, and understanding search's incremental contribution within the broader media mix. This role will report to our Director of Growth. You will own and scale our search presence across both paid and organic. You'll be our in-house expert across Google Ads, Microsoft Ads and organic search strategy. This is a high-ownership role sitting at the intersection of performance and content.




WHAT YOU’LL DO:

Paid Search

 - Own strategy, execution, and optimization of paid search campaigns across Google Ads and Microsoft Ads (Shopping, Search, Performance Max, YouTube, Demand Gen)

 - Manage and allocate a multi-million dollar search budget to hit customer acquisition cost targets

 - Build and maintain a rigorous testing cadence around keywords, bidding strategies, ad copy & landing pages

 - Own feed management and segmentation opportunities

 - Stay ahead of platform changes (Demand Gen, SmartAI, PMAX evolution) and bring forward-looking recommendations



SEO

 - Own organic search strategy across technical SEO, on-page optimization, and content

 - Develop and execute a content strategy targeting high-intent keywords across the eyewear space

 - Build and manage a link acquisition program to grow domain authority over time



Cross-Channel

 - Partner with the data & analytics team to build reporting in Looker and ensure search data (paid + organic) integrates cleanly into our internal views

 - Collaborate with the creative team to align search messaging & asset evaluation across our campaigns

 - Coordinate with paid social and affiliate to align channel strategy, share keyword intelligence, and avoid cannibalization




WHO YOU ARE:

 - You're a full-stack search expert who owns both paid and organic with confidence

 - You're analytical and can making decisions from data and translating performance into clear, actionable insights

 - You're a strong cross-functional collaborator, comfortable working with other teams to get things done

 - You're a strong executioner, excited to develop scalable processes and systems as the channel grows

 - You move fast and prioritize well, balancing long-term strategy with the day-to-day demands of a high-growth DTC environment




NEED TO HAVE:

 - 6+ years of hands-on experience in paid search and SEO, ideally within DTC or e-commerce

 - Deep fluency in Google Ads, Google Merchant Center, and Microsoft Ads — you can build and QA campaigns yourself with expertise within Google Ads Editor

 - Experience managing $3M+ in annual search spend with demonstrable efficiency gains

 - Strong understanding of incrementality, attribution, and how paid and organic search fit into a broader media mix

 - Comfortable assessing and analyzing data within Looker



Nice to Have:

 - Experience with subscription or membership products

 - Background managing search for licensed partnerships or co-branded collaborations

 - Familiarity with weekly or high-cadence product drop environments

 - Experience with both brand and performance search strategies

 - Experience leveraging AI tools to accelerate testing velocity and workflow efficiency




SOME BENEFITS AND PERKS OF WORKING AT PAIR:

 - Health, vision and dental insurance

 - Generous vacation policies

 - Competitive salaries with equity opportunities

 - Retirement savings plan options

 - Free eyewear (plus discounts for family and frie

source_jobs.title: Senior Data Analyst
source_jobs.description:
At Pair Eyewear, we believe in placing our people at the center of everything we do. We are dedicated to fostering a joyful and dynamic workplace, providing our employees with an environment where they can thrive and make a meaningful impact.



Pair is seeking a Senior Data Analyst that will report directly to our Senior Manager of Data & Analytics. This individual should be analytical, strategic, and impact driven. This is an opportunity to work cross-functionally with various teams in the company to elevate the organization by uncovering trends and opportunities, decreasing the time to insight, and making data accessible to all.



What You’ll Do:

 - Collaborate with stakeholders in our growth marketing, merchandising, digital product, finance, wholesale and operations departments to define business questions and identify opportunities for data-driven improvements

 - Play a critical role in attacking opaque, unstructured problems; develop core analytic frameworks that shape the way the company thinks about our business

 - Dive head first into our data to uncover insights that can improve the customer experience, drive revenue, or cut costs for the business

 - Document and communicate data insights and recommendations to stakeholders across the company and advocate for a data-driven culture internally and externally

 - Build a data-driven culture throughout the organization to ensure that all decisions accurately utilize the most powerful data available

 - Work cross-functionally to upgrade and optimize our reporting framework in our BI tools and measure initiatives' impact by tracking KPIs



Who You Are:

 - Self-starter who has a great work ethic, analytical mind, and superior problem-solving and decision-making skills

 - Excellent communication skills - someone who values building relationships and enjoys explaining complex topics in simple terms

 - Proven experience analyzing and interpreting large sets of data in eCommerce, consumer packaged goods, GTM, sales, marketing, demand planning, sales & operations planning, supply chain, or manufacturing



Need to Have:

 - 5+ years of experience in quantitative analysis or data science roles

 - Advanced skills in SQL, Python and dbt

 - Experience creating dashboards in BI tools such as Hex, Looker, or others

 - Experience working in modern data warehouse environments, e.g. Snowflake, Amazon Redshift, Google BigQuery or similar



Nice to Have:

 - Experience with marketing attribution methods and building LTV or MMM models



Some Benefits and Perks of Working at Pair:

 - Health, vision and dental insurance

 - Generous vacation policies

 - Competitive salaries with equity opportunities

 - Retirement savings plan options

 - Free eyewear (plus discounts for family and friends)

 - NYC dog friendly office with flexible hybrid work options (if you’re local in NYC)



At Pair, we carefully consider a wide range of factors when determining compensation. These considerations can cause your compensation to vary. The pay range for this position is expected to be between $120,000-$150,000 and will depend on your skills, qualifications, and experience. Additionally, this role might be eligible for discretionary short-term and long-term incentives. We encourage all interested candidates to apply.

Pair is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment and will not be discriminated against on the basis of actual or perceived age, ancestry, citizenship, color, disability, familial status, family responsibilities, gender identity/expression, genetic information, marital status, matriculation, national origin, personal appearance, political affiliation, pregnancy or related condition (including breastfeeding), race, religion, sex, sexual orientation, socioeconomic status, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal

source_jobs.title: Outside Sales Representative, Optical
source_jobs.description:
At Pair Eyewear, we believe in placing our people at the center of everything we do. We are dedicated to fostering a joyful and dynamic workplace, providing our employees with an environment where they can thrive and make a meaningful impact.



We're looking for a driven, relationship-first Sales Representative to join our team. In this role, you'll own a territory with a dual focus: opening new doors and deepening our presence within existing accounts. You're not just an account executive you're a strategic partner to every account you touch.



The ideal candidate comes from the optical industry, understands how buying decisions get made with independent optical retailers, and knows how to turn a cold intro into a long-term partnership. You're comfortable with the numbers, confident in prospecting and skilled at working through objections without losing the relationship.



What You’ll Do:

Business Development — New Door Penetration

 - Identify, prospect, and close new accounts within your assigned territory

 - Build a consistent pipeline through cold outreach, referrals, and field activity

 - Develop and execute a territory plan to maximize market coverage

 - Represent the brand at trade shows, regional events, and in-office visits



Account Management

 - Create a strategic sales cycle with your accounts based on growth potential and overall value.

 - Serve as the primary point of contact for existing accounts

 - Build deep relationships with key decision-makers (practice owners, opticians, buyers)

 - Drive product adoption and increase share of wallet within active accounts

 - Conduct regular business reviews and ensure accounts are set up for success



Strategy & Analysis

 - Achieve KPIs through our performance dashboards and account playbooks.

 - Analyze sales data to identify trends, opportunities, and gaps in your territory

 - Develop account-specific strategies to grow revenue and improve retention

 - Track and report on pipeline activity, forecasts, and performance metrics

 - Identify operational or product feedback from accounts to help inform cross-functional improvements.

 - Partner with internal teams (marketing, product, operations) to address account needs



Objection Handling & Consultative Selling

 - Lead confident, consultative conversations that uncover customer needs

 - Address pricing, product, and competitive objections with clarity and confidence

 - Adapt messaging based on account type, buyer persona, and stage in the sales cycle



Who You Are:

 - A relationship builder who enjoys creating long-term partnerships with customers

 - Self-motivated and comfortable managing a territory independently

 - Results-oriented with a strong sense of ownership and accountability

 - Confident in prospecting and business development activities

 - Analytical and data-driven when developing sales strategies

 - Skilled at navigating objections and complex customer conversations

 - Organized, proactive, and able to effectively prioritize competing responsibilities

 - A strong communicator who can build credibility with a variety of stakeholders



Need to Have:

 - 3–6+ years of outside sales, territory sales, or account management experience

 - Proven track record of prospecting, opening new accounts, and growing territory revenue

 - Demonstrated success achieving or exceeding sales goals

 - Experience within the optical industry, including independent practices, optical chains, or wholesale eyewear

 - Experience managing a full sales cycle from prospecting through close

 - Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret sales data and develop territory strategies

 - Experience using CRM platforms to manage pipelines, customer activity, and forecasting

 - Excellent communication, presentation, and relationship-building skills

 - Ability to travel regularly throughout an assigned territory



Nice to Have:

 - Experience selling eyewear, optical frames, lenses, or related vis

source_jobs.title: Eyewear Designer
source_jobs.description:
At Pair Eyewear, we believe in placing our people at the center of everything we do. We are dedicated to fostering a joyful and dynamic workplace, providing our employees with an environment where they can thrive and make a meaningful impact.



Pair Eyewear is looking for a frame designer to own the look, feel, and design identity of our eyewear. This is a creative, concept-driven role: you’ll define the aesthetic direction of our frames and bring new shapes and collections to life through sketching, illustration, and hand-drawing. You’ll set the design vision and partner with our development team to take concepts toward production. If you have an eye for form, a point of view on style, and a love of eyewear, we want to see your work.


What You’ll Do:

 - Define the look, feel, and design identity of Pair’s frames — from overall aesthetic direction to the details of each shape.

 - Conceptualize and develop new frame designs through sketching, illustration, hand-drawing, and digital rendering.

 - Explore form, proportion, silhouette, and style across the collection.

 - Direct materials, color, and finish (CMF) — selecting and combining acetate, metal, and mixed materials to express each design.

 - Develop balanced, on-trend collections aligned with the Pair brand and the product roadmap.

 - Partner with the product development team to carry concepts from idea toward production, keeping the design intent intact.

 - Research eyewear, fashion, and materials trends, and study competitor product, to keep the line fresh and distinct.

 - Present design concepts and direction clearly to cross-functional partners.



Who You Are:

 - A frame designer with a strong point of view on style, form, and what makes eyewear feel special.

 - A skilled illustrator — confident in hand-drawing and digital sketching to communicate ideas quickly and beautifully.

 - Trend-aware and curious, with a feel for where eyewear and fashion are heading.

 - Organized and self-driven, able to run multiple designs in parallel against a product calendar.

 - Collaborative — you enjoy working with development, category, and brand partners to bring designs to life.



Need to Have:

 - Direct eyewear industry experience designing frames.

 - Degree in Industrial Design, Product Design, Fashion Design, or a related field — design school strongly preferred. Equivalent professional experience considered.

 - Strong hand-drawing, illustration, and digital sketching skills (e.g. Procreate, Photoshop, Illustrator).

 - A strong sense of form, proportion, brand aesthetic, and color/material/finish.

 - Working knowledge of eyewear materials and construction — enough to design with manufacturability in mind.

 - A portfolio showing original eyewear / frame design, from concept through finished product.



Nice to Have:

 - Experience designing for a direct-to-consumer/ wholesale retail or high-volume eyewear brand.

 - Specialized eyewear-design training or apprenticeships.

 - A background in trend or CMF research.

 - Familiarity with optical basics and prescription frame considerations.



Some Benefits and Perks of Working At Pair:

 - Medical, vision, and dental insurance

 - Generous vacation policy

 - Retirement savings plan options

 - Free eyewear (plus discounts for family and friends)



At Pair, we carefully consider a wide range of factors when determining compensation. These considerations can cause your compensation to vary. The pay range for this position is expected to be between $170,000 - $180,000 and will depend on your skills, qualifications, and experience. Additionally, this role might be eligible for discretionary short-term and long-term incentives. We encourage all interested candidates to apply.

Pair is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment and will not be discriminated against on the basis of actual or perceived age, ancestry, citizenship, color, disability, familial status

notes: Pair Eyewear is a fashion/eyewear company with a mix of creative, sales, and analytical roles.
